title: cerastium fontanum var. vulgare
intro: Cerastium fontanum var. vulgare, also known as Field Chickweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the carnation family, characterized by its small white flowers and oval-shaped leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Field Chickweed include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Field Chickweed are typically made into teas, infusions, or ointments, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
